description:
Create an Average intensity projection and a Median projection of a 10-frame FITS image sequence in AstroImageJ, subtract the Median from the Average to create a diagnostic residual map, and report the extreme maximum and minimum pixel values of the residuals.

The 10 calibrated FITS frames are located in `~/AstroImages/stack_diagnostics/frames/`. Import them as an Image Sequence (File > Import > Image Sequence...).

1. Create an Average Intensity projection (Image > Stacks > Z Project...) and save it as `~/AstroImages/stack_diagnostics/results/average_stack.fits`.
2. Create a Median projection and save it as `~/AstroImages/stack_diagnostics/results/median_stack.fits`.
3. Use the Image Calculator (Process > Image Calculator...) to subtract the Median image from the Average image (Operation: Subtract, Image1: Average, Image2: Median), ensuring the result is a 32-bit float image so negative residual values are not truncated (check '32-bit (float) result').
4. Save this residual image as `~/AstroImages/stack_diagnostics/results/diagnostic_residuals.fits`.
5. Measure the maximum positive pixel value and minimum negative pixel value across the entire residual image (e.g., using Analyze > Measure on the whole image or checking the Histogram).
6. Create a text report at `~/AstroImages/stack_diagnostics/results/diagnostics_report.txt` containing exactly:
